section.title: Purpose
section.body: This SOP defines the mandatory process for notifying and preparing the Payments Team and Customer Service (CS) Team whenever the Marketing Team launches or schedules a promotional campaign. Its goals are to ensure operational readiness, minimise risk exposure, detect bonus abuse early, and deliver a consistent player experience across all touchpoints.

section.title: Scope
section.body: Applies to all marketing campaigns including but not limited to:

items.item: Welcome and sign-up bonuses
Reload and deposit-match promotions
Free spins, free bets, and no-deposit offers
Loyalty, VIP, and tiered reward campaigns
Seasonal or event-based promotions (e.g. tournaments, jackpots)
Referral and affiliate-driven campaigns
Retargeting and re-engagement campaigns

responsibilities.item: Owns the campaign brief and initiates cross-team notification
Ensures the Campaign Brief Pack is complete and distributed on time
Monitors for bonus abuse patterns and coordinates a cross-team response
Holds authority to pause or terminate the campaign if abuse or risk thresholds are breached
Chairs pre-launch sign-off and post-campaign debrief meetings
Receives briefing and configures deposit rules for the campaign period
Briefs Payments agents on campaign mechanics and escalation path
Oversees real-time transaction monitoring throughout the campaign
Escalates anomalies and fraud patterns to the Marketing Manager immediately
Compiles post-campaign transaction report
Apply campaign-specific deposit rules correctly
Monitor higher-value transactions in real time
Log and escalate any flagged accounts or suspicious patterns to the Payments Lead
Receives briefing and trains CS agents on promotion FAQs before launch
Ensures consistent and accurate query resolution across the team
Escalates unresolved complaints and abuse flags to the Marketing Manager
Compiles post-campaign CS query summary
Answer all player queries on the promotion accurately and consistently
Follow approved FAQ scripts; never speculate outside approved content
Flag suspected player abuse patterns to the CS Lead immediately
Escalate complaints, mis-sell allegations, and regulatory threats promptly
Provides live reporting dashboards for the duration of the campaign
Flags unusual behavioural or financial metrics to the Marketing Manager

section.title: Notification and Briefing Timeline
section.body: All briefings must be completed before the campaign goes live. The following minimum lead times are mandatory.

timeline.milestone: Marketing shares the Campaign Brief Pack with the Payments Lead and CS Lead
Payments Lead confirms deposit thresholds, monitoring plan, and abuse detection setup
CS Lead distributes the FAQ document and runs the team briefing session
Final confirmation call and sign-off across all three teams
Payments monitoring activated; CS agents ready; Marketing confirms go-live
Initial performance and incident check-in meeting
Full campaign health review; escalate any unresolved issues
Post-campaign debrief; SOP improvement notes recorded

section.title: Campaign Brief Pack (Mandatory Document)
section.body: Marketing must produce and distribute a Campaign Brief Pack for every campaign. The pack must include all of the following sections.

items.item: Campaign name and unique reference ID
Campaign type (bonus, free spins, referral, reload, etc.)
Start date, end date, and active hours with time zone
Targeted player segments (new, existing, VIP, geography)
Channels used (email, SMS, push notification, paid social, affiliate)
Estimated reach and expected uplift in player activity
Full Terms and Conditions — plain-English summary and legal version
Trigger conditions (minimum deposit, opt-in required, promo code)
Bonus value, structure, and award timing
Wagering requirements and game restrictions
Maximum withdrawal from bonus winnings
Expiry periods for bonus credits and free spins
Exclusions (payment methods, countries, player segments)
Approved FAQs and scripted answers for CS agents
Links to landing page, banner creative, and email template
Key messaging and tone guidance (what to say and what not to say)
Escalation wording for players unhappy with the promotion
Defined bonus abuse indicators for this campaign (e.g. structuring deposits, rapid withdrawal post-bonus)
Maximum number of promotional accounts per household or device
Payment methods excluded from the promotion
Velocity thresholds that trigger a manual review
Escalation contact within Marketing for confirmed abuse cases
Review Campaign Brief Pack and confirm deposit bonus thresholds
Configure or verify payment system rules for the promotion period
Set up real-time monitoring alerts based on abuse risk parameters provided by Marketing
Brief all Payments agents on campaign mechanics and escalation path

subsection.title: During the Campaign – Transaction Monitoring
subsection.body: The Payments Team is specifically responsible for monitoring higher-value deposit activity throughout the campaign period.

items.item: Track unusual spikes in deposit volume or average deposit value against baseline
Identify players making multiple deposits at or near bonus trigger thresholds (structuring behaviour)
Flag accounts depositing via multiple payment methods in a short window
Monitor for rapid bonus-to-withdrawal patterns
Report all flagged activity to the Marketing Manager without delay

escalation_rule.trigger: Transaction volumes exceed [X]% of baseline within a 2-hour window
escalation_rule.action: Notify Marketing Manager immediately via phone and #campaigns-live channel
Log in Incident Register and notify Marketing Manager within 30 minutes

escalation_rule.trigger: Confirmed or suspected multi-accounting or promotional abuse
escalation_rule.action: Notify Marketing Manager immediately; Marketing Manager decides on campaign action

notes.item: All escalations logged in the Incident Register within 30 minutes of detection
Marketing Manager owns the abuse investigation and any decisions to restrict accounts or pause the campaign

items.item: Compile transaction volume report for the full campaign period
Document all flagged and investigated accounts
Submit summary findings to Marketing Manager within 2 business days of campaign end
CS Lead reviews Campaign Brief Pack and raises any ambiguities with Marketing before launch
CS Lead drafts or approves the player-facing FAQ document (template from Marketing)
Mandatory team briefing session held at least 1 working day before launch
All CS agents complete a knowledge check on campaign mechanics
Escalation contact for complex queries confirmed and circulated to the team

subsection.title: During the Campaign – Query Handling
subsection.body: CS agents must be equipped to handle all promotion-related player queries, including:

items.item: How to qualify (eligibility, opt-in steps, promo codes)
When and how the bonus or free spins will be credited
Wagering requirements and which games count towards them
Withdrawal conditions and maximum bonus winnings
What to do if a bonus has not appeared as expected
Promotion expiry and consequences of not using the bonus in time
Why a player may have been excluded from the promotion

subsection.title: Tone and Accuracy Standards
subsection.golden_rule: Agents must never speculate or provide information not in the approved FAQ. If unsure, confirm with the CS Lead before responding. Incorrect information given to players about promotions constitutes an operational and reputational risk.

items.item: All responses must align with approved campaign wording
Agents must not make exceptions to campaign T&Cs without authorisation from the CS Lead
Any player complaint about the fairness of a promotion must be escalated to the CS Lead

subsection.title: Abuse Indicators – CS Observations
subsection.body: CS agents are often the first point of contact with players attempting to exploit a promotion. Agents must flag the following behaviours to the CS Lead immediately, who will escalate to the Marketing Manager:

items.item: Player enquiring about how to use multiple accounts to access the promotion
Player asking whether they can transfer a bonus to another account
Player with a pattern of claiming bonuses and immediately requesting withdrawals
Multiple contacts from the same device, IP, or household regarding the same promotion
Player attempting to circumvent an exclusion from the promotion

escalation_rule.trigger: Player has not received bonus after meeting all stated conditions
escalation_rule.action: Escalate to Payments Lead if unresolved within 24 hours

escalation_rule.trigger: Player alleges the promotion was mis-sold or misleading
escalation_rule.action: Escalate to CS Lead and Marketing Manager
Escalate to CS Lead and Marketing Manager immediately; document in full
CS Lead to notify Marketing Manager; assess whether FAQ update is required

items.item: CS Lead compiles summary of promotion-related queries: volume, top issues, resolution rate
Flag any recurring ambiguities in campaign T&Cs for future improvement
Submit CS summary report to Marketing Manager within 2 business days of campaign end

section.title: Bonus Abuse Management (Marketing-Led)
section.body: The Marketing Team owns bonus abuse detection and response for all campaigns. Payments and CS feed intelligence upward; Marketing investigates and acts.

items.item: Payments Team: transaction monitoring alerts and flagged account reports
CS Team: player behaviour observations reported during live support interactions
CRM / BI dashboards: unusual conversion spikes, deposit patterns, and withdrawal velocity
Multiple accounts per player (multi-accounting) claiming the same bonus
Structured deposits designed to trigger bonus thresholds repeatedly
Immediate or near-immediate withdrawal following bonus clearance
Coordinated group abuse (gnoming) across linked accounts
Use of excluded payment methods to circumvent promotion restrictions
Arbitrage play — targeting low house-edge games to meet wagering requirements
Review flagged accounts against campaign eligibility criteria
Void bonuses for accounts in confirmed breach of T&Cs
Restrict future promotional eligibility for confirmed abusers
Pause or modify campaign parameters if systemic abuse is detected
Document all abuse cases in the Abuse Log for pattern analysis
Update campaign T&Cs and abuse risk parameters for future campaigns based on findings

channel.method: Email to all leads plus shared drive folder (link in email)
Video call — calendar invite sent by Marketing Manager
Phone call to Marketing Manager plus message in #campaigns-live
Email to Marketing Manager plus upload to shared drive
Incident Register and Abuse Log (shared documents — links in brief pack)

section.title: Incident Management
section.body: Any operational issue during a campaign — technical fault, unusual player behaviour, abuse pattern, or significant query spike — must be treated as a campaign incident.

steps.step: Detecting team (Payments or CS) logs the incident in the Incident Register immediately
Team Lead notifies Marketing Manager via phone and #campaigns-live within 30 minutes
Marketing Manager convenes an urgent cross-team call within 1 hour of notification
Decision made to continue, modify, or pause the campaign based on severity
All decisions and actions documented in the Incident Register
Post-incident review held within 3 business days; learnings fed back into this SOP

section.title: Post-Campaign Debrief
section.body: A debrief meeting must be held within 5 working days of campaign close. Attendees: Marketing Manager, Payments Lead, CS Lead.

agenda_items.item: Campaign performance vs. targets (impressions, conversions, deposit uplift)
Payments review: transaction volumes, flagged accounts, confirmed abuse cases
CS review: query volume, top issues, resolution rate, player sentiment
Abuse review: patterns detected, actions taken, T&C gaps identified
Lessons learned and recommended SOP updates

section.note: Debrief notes must be saved to the shared campaign archive folder. Agreed process improvements must be reflected in this SOP within 10 working days.

section.title: Document Control and Version History
section.review_cycle: This SOP must be reviewed and re-approved every 12 months, or immediately following any significant campaign incident. Owner: Marketing Manager.

entry.summary: Initial version. Marketing-led abuse management. No separate compliance role.
